narration: Define a helper function to merge two sorted arrays into a single sorted array.
Initialize an empty list called 'sorted_list' to store the merged result.
Initialize two pointers, 'i' and 'j', to zero to track the current index of the first and second arrays respectively.
Create a while loop that continues as long as both pointers are within the bounds of their respective arrays.
Inside the loop, compare the element at index 'i' of the first array with the element at index 'j' of the second array.
If the element from the first array is smaller or equal, append it to 'sorted_list' and increment pointer 'i'.
Otherwise, append the element from the second array to 'sorted_list' and increment pointer 'j'.
After the main loop exits, add a loop to append any remaining elements from the first array to 'sorted_list'.
Add a second loop to append any remaining elements from the second array to 'sorted_list'.
Define the main 'merge_sort' function that takes an unsorted array as input.
Implement the base case: if the array length is less than or equal to one, return the array immediately.
Calculate the middle index of the array using integer division.
Split the array into two halves: 'left' (from start to mid) and 'right' (from mid to end).
Recursively call 'merge_sort' on the 'left' half and store the result.
Recursively call 'merge_sort' on the 'right' half and store the result.
Call the merge helper function with the sorted 'left' and 'right' arrays and return the result.
Refactor the merge function to sort in-place by passing the original array and using an index 'k' for assignment instead of appending.
